content.problemStatement:
You are a marketing analyst at MaxLinear tasked with identifying customers who use specific email providers for a targeted email campaign.

Task**Your goal is to:**


Extract a list of customers who use popular generic email services
Include only customers with emails from gmail.com, yahoo.com, or hotmail.com domains
Return each customer's ID, first name, and last name
Exclude customers using company or other email domains
Table StructureThe `customers` table contains:


`customer_id:` Unique identifier for each customer
`first_name:` Customer's first name
`last_name:` Customer's last name
`email:` Customer's email address
Customers Datacustomer_idfirst_namelast_nameemail6125JohnDoejohndoe@gmail.com7814EmilyClarkemily.clark@yahoo.com5329AmandaTayloramanda.taylor@maxlinear.com6491JacobMillerjacob_miller@hotmail.com4578SophiaJohnsonsophia.johnson@gmail.com
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of customers (1 ≤ T ≤ 1000). Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated fields: customer_id (integer), first_name (string), last_name (string) and email (string). Email contains exactly one '@' character and has no spaces.
content.outputFormat: For every customer whose email domain is exactly one of "gmail.com", "yahoo.com" or "hotmail.com" (case‑sensitive), output a line with three space‑separated values: customer_id first_name last_name. Preserve the original order of appearance. If no customer matches, output nothing.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
6125 John Doe johndoe@gmail.com
7814 Emily Clark emily.clark@yahoo.com
5329 Amanda Taylor amanda.taylor@maxlinear.com
sampleTestCases.explanation: The first two customers have email domains gmail.com and yahoo.com, which are in the allowed list. The third customer's domain is maxlinear.com, so it is excluded.

sampleTestCases.input: 2\n6491 Jacob Miller jacob_miller@hotmail.com\n4578 Sophia Johnson sophia.johnson@gmail.com
sampleTestCases.explanation: Both customers use allowed domains (hotmail.com and gmail.com), therefore both lines appear in the output.

sampleTestCases.input:
5
1001 Alice Brown alice.brown@outlook.com
1002 Bob White bob.white@yahoo.com
1003 Carol Green carol@HOTMAIL.COM
1004 Dave Black dave.black@gmail.com
1005 Eve Gray eve.gray@company.org
sampleTestCases.explanation: Domain comparison is case‑sensitive. Only bob.white@yahoo.com and dave.black@gmail.com match exactly the lower‑case allowed domains. carol@HOTMAIL.COM is uppercase and therefore excluded.

hidden.input:
15
9999 Zeta Alpha zeta@maxlinear.com
10000 Omega Beta omega@yahoo.com
1234 Gamma Delta gamma.delta@gmail.com
5678 Theta Epsilon theta_epsilon@hotmail.com
4321 Iota Zeta iota@company.org
8765 Kappa Lambda kappa.lambda@GMAIL.COM
1111 Mu Nu mu.nu@yahoo.com
2222 Xi Omicron xi@outlook.com
3333 Pi Rho pi.rho@hotmail.com
4444 Sigma Tau sigma.tau@gmail.com

hidden.input:
5
101 John Doe john.doe@gmail.com
102 Jane Smith jane.smith@gmail.com
103 Bob Brown bob.brown@gmail.com
104 Alice White alice.white@gmail.com
105 Tom Black tom.black@gmail.com

102 Jane Smith

hidden.input:
4
2001 A B a@mail.gmail.com
2002 C D c@yahoo.com
2003 E F e@hot.mail.com
2004 G H g@GMAIL.COM

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.input:
8
3001 Anna Lee anna.lee@yahoo.com
3002 Brian Kim brian.kim@maxlinear.com
3003 Carla Gomez carla.gomez@hotmail.com
3004 David Park david.park@gmail.com
3005 Eva Liu eva.liu@outlook.com
3006 Frank Wu frank.wu@yahoo.com
3007 Grace Ho grace.ho@Hotmail.com
3008 Henry Yu henry.yu@gmail.com
hidden.expectedOutput:
3001 Anna Lee
3003 Carla Gomez
3004 David Park
3006 Frank Wu
3008 Henry Yu

solution.approach: Read the integer T. Loop T times reading the four fields. Locate the '@' character in the email string; the substring after it is the domain. Compare the domain with the three allowed strings ("gmail.com", "yahoo.com", "hotmail.com") using exact (case‑sensitive) equality. If it matches, immediately output the customer's id, first name and last name preserving input order. The algorithm runs in O(T) time and uses O(1) extra space besides the input storage.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, where T is the number of customers.
